behavior_summary: Implements a telepad's trigger/activation behavior: when an actor activates the pad the script advances a short animation over several trigger resumptions and, once the animation reaches a terminal frame, performs a teleport/effect using coordinates and stored pad data.

The routine runs in an event-driven form (fast-area trigger): it sets area info and suspends, resumes when the trigger is activated, reads the pad's frame and coordinates, and either increments the frame and spawns a transient effect or calls a FREE routine to execute the teleport action.

Advancing the animation involves incrementing the pad's frame via Item::setFrame and spawning an auxiliary handler (FREE::1C19) with a small event code; the teleport branch calls FREE::2411 with X/Y/Z, an effect id (0x034F), the item pointer, and a 2-byte value read from the item (likely a destination id).
notable_control_flow: Entry via a fast-area trigger — the script sets trigger info and suspends, yielding until the pad's area is activated (enterFastArea semantics).

On each resume it reads Item::getFrame(item). If frame < 3 the script increments the frame, calls Item::setFrame(item, frame+1), spawns FREE::1C19 (likely an effect), then re-suspends to wait for further trigger resumes (loop).

When Item::getFrame(item) == 3 the script executes the teleport/effect path by reading Item::getX/Y/Z(item) and calling FREE::2411 with coordinates, a fixed effect id (0x034F), the item pointer and an indirect 2-byte value from the item, then terminates/returns.
open_questions: What exact gameplay semantics does the 2-byte indirect value read from the item represent (destination id, map id, linked telepad id, or other metadata)? — inferred but not certain.
What is the canonical meaning of the constants passed to FREE::2411 (0x034F) and FREE::1C19 (0x0A)? Are they effect ids, spell ids, event codes, or spawn-type identifiers?
Which actor/player PID is passed to FREE::1C19 when advancing the frame (inference: the triggering actor's pid) and how is it sourced in the broader engine context?

Does the telepad behavior require staying inside the fast-area for multiple resumption events (enter/leave semantics) or is each activation a discrete trigger? The code implies repeated resumes while the pad animates but the exact trigger lifecycle is implementation-dependent.
Does FREE::2411 perform a full-map teleport, summon, or some other 'free' engine action? The name and args strongly suggest teleporting but the exact side-effects are inferred.

main_function.name: U8P_TELEPAD::0080
main_function.purpose: Handle a telepad item's activation: advance its local animation across trigger resumptions and, when the animation reaches the final frame, perform the teleport/effect using the pad's coordinates and stored destination metadata.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the telepad's fast-area trigger is set/activated (enterFastArea-style trigger); the routine sets area info and suspends until activation.
Runs repeatedly on resume from the suspended state to progress animation or execute the teleport when the final frame is reached.

function_map.name: U8P_TELEPAD::0080
function_map.role: Telepad fast-area activation handler that advances animation frames and triggers the teleport/effect when the final frame is reached.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Read the telepad item's current animation/frame index (used to decide whether to advance animation or teleport).

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Write a new frame value to the telepad item (used to advance the animation by +1).

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Read telepad X coordinate for use when invoking the teleport/effect routine.

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Read telepad Y coordinate for use when invoking the teleport/effect routine.

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Read telepad Z coordinate for use when invoking the teleport/effect routine.

function_map.name: FREE::2411
function_map.role: Engine routine invoked to perform the teleport/effect using coordinates, an effect id (0x034F), the item pointer, and an indirect 2-byte item value (likely destination metadata).

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawn auxiliary effect/handler on animation advance (likely visual/sound/timer) with arguments including pid, event/code 0x0A and 0.

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the telepad's current animation/frame to decide whether to advance the animation or perform the teleport.
Set the telepad's animation frame to frame+1 when advancing the animation on activation.
Spawn a short-lived auxiliary process/effect (likely sound/visual/timer) when the pad frame is advanced.
Read pad X coordinate to pass to the teleport/effect routine.
Read pad Y coordinate to pass to the teleport/effect routine.
Read pad Z coordinate to pass to the teleport/effect routine.

Execute the teleport/effect action when the pad reaches its final animation frame; passes coordinates, a zero byte, effect id 0x034F, the item pointer, and a 2-byte value read from the item (likely destination id).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements a telepad's trigger/activation behavior: when an actor activates the pad the script advances a short animation over several trigger resumptions and, once the animation reaches a terminal frame, performs a teleport/effect using coordinates and stored pad data.
